data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: Design principle used to increase usability by optimizing click targets.
data.technical_spec: Interactive elements are strategically placed and sized for easier targeting and click accuracy.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Streamlines decision-making by reducing choice overload in user interfaces.
data.technical_spec: Simplifies the user journey by presenting only necessary options to avoid decision paralysis.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Design consistency across platforms increases user familiarity and trust.
data.technical_spec: Maintains uniform design patterns for consistent user experiences across all devices.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Ensures user memory capacity is not overwhelmed by limiting data chunking.
data.technical_spec: Divides information into manageable chunks to enhance content comprehension and retention.

data.title: Aesthetic Usability Effect
data.description: Users perceive aesthetically pleasing design as more usable.
data.technical_spec: Prioritizes visual design elements to enhance aesthetics while maintaining functional usability.
